mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack signals severe economic weakness and depressed corporate earnings, causing investors to price in recession risks and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive forward equity returns during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during late-cycle expansions, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with industrial production growth outside the recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth serves as a comprehensive measure of aggregate economic activity, where higher growth rates directly correlate with stronger corporate earnings trajectories and sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 55% of high-inflation trigger instances, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and a declining federal funds rate creates a favorable liquidity and employment environment, which amplifies risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when payrolls decline, or the joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines in out-of-sample testing over a 2-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 700 thousand units, it indicates a severe housing market contraction that drags down construction spending and consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns during housing downturns.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or predictive accuracy drops below chance when housing inventory levels remain historically low.
